channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Jill Biden says she is closing the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: Jill Biden has announced that she is closing the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction because of Supreme Court immunity ruling
item.description: A judge has refused to dismiss President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court’s recent ruling on presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: RFK Jr. meets with senators as questions swirl about Trump's pick to lead health agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. swept onto Capitol Hill late Monday as the anti-vaccine health guru from the famous political family reintroduced himself to senators, this time as President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the nation's Health and Human Services Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he might reverse President Joe Biden’s decision to permit Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Huffman wins key vote to succeed Grijalva atop Natural Resources Democrats
item.description: Rep. Jared Huffman (D-Calif.) won a Democratic Steering and Policy Committee vote Monday to succeed Rep. Raul Grijalva (D-Ariz.) as the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, advancing him to a full vote of the Democratic caucus.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two charged in connection with Iran-backed drone strike that killed 3 US troops in the Middle East
item.description: Two men, including a dual Iranian American citizen, have been arrested on charges that they exported sensitive technology to Iran that was used in a drone attack in Jordan that killed three American troops early this year and injured dozens of other servicemembers.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic panel recommends Craig for top seat on Agriculture Committee
item.description: A key Democratic panel voted Monday to recommend Rep. Angie Craig (D-Minn.) to be the top Democrat on the House Agriculture Committee in the next Congress, lending a big boost to the most junior lawmaker in the contest heading into a deciding vote of the full caucus on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A former FBI informant pleaded guilty on Monday to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son Hunter that became central to the Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Judges backing out of retirement ahead of Trump term leave GOP senators fuming
item.description: Republicans have expressed frustration after several judges are announcing unretirements that would prevent Trump from appointing their successors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court won’t block order directing Peter Navarro to turn over emails
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to weigh Peter Navarro’s bid to reverse a court order directing him to turn over hundreds of emails sent during his tenure as an adviser in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Biden, Harris thank major Democratic donors and urge them to stay engaged after tough loss to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris on Sunday thanked deep-pocketed Democratic donors who raised record sums in last month's election loss to President-elect Donald Trump and urged them not to lose hope and to remain politically engaged.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: Trump plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Momentum builds around Social Security reforms ahead of Senate vote
item.description: Momentum is building around legislation that could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans as Senate leadership tees up a vote for next week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Pelosi hospitalized in Luxembourg after sustaining injury from fall on stairs
item.description: Former Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.) was hospitalized for evaluation in Luxembourg after falling on a set of stairs and sustaining an injury during a trip with a congressional delegation.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to know about Biden’s 1,500 clemencies, pardons
item.description: President Biden pardoned 39 people and commuted the sentences of nearly 1,500 others on Thursday, a sweeping move that comes after pressure from criminal justice advocates over the surprise pardon of his son Hunter Biden.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: These are the Trump appointees who have faced legal troubles
item.description: President-elect Trump has named several people to his next administration who have faced legal troubles — including Trump himself, who was convicted in a New York hush money case during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Trump invites China's Xi to next month's inauguration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is breaking with precedent in inviting a foreign head of state to his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: READ: Here is Biden's full list of clemencies
item.description: President Biden early Thursday announced that he is pardoning 39 people and commuting the sentences of nearly 1,500 others, setting a new daily record for clemency with a focus on those who were under home confinement during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Balluck, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis City Council overrides mayor’s veto of 2025 budget
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council enacted its 2025 budget on Thursday, after overturning a veto by Mayor Jacob Frey.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump is named Time's Person of the Year, will ring the New York Stock Exchange bell
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is back in his hometown of New York where he's been honored as Time magazine’s Person of the Year and will ring the opening bell at the New York Stock Exchange.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
